About

Turcsik
Bella

Graphic Designer — Budapest

Photo coming soon
Background

Bella studied graphic design in Budapest, where she developed a practice that draws equally from European modernism and contemporary visual culture.

Her work encompasses brand identity systems for cultural institutions and independent businesses, conceptual illustration, and typographic editorial design. She believes that the best visual communication is precise enough to be clear, and open enough to be felt.

When not designing, she is studying colour relationships, reading about the history of the poster, and walking around the city with her camera.

Based in

Budapest, Hungary

Available for

Freelance & collaboration

Education

Graphic Design, Budapest

Work Together
Influences
01

René Magritte

The art of the impossible ordinary

His work taught me that the most unsettling — and beautiful — images are those that place familiar objects in unfamiliar relationships. Precision as a tool for poetry.

02

Gustav Klimt

Pattern as emotion

The way he dissolves the boundary between ornament and figure, between background and subject. Decoration is never superficial — it is meaning made visible.

03

Bauhaus

Form follows function, and beauty follows both

A reminder that constraint is generative. The grid is not a cage — it is a scaffold for clarity. Typography, colour, and geometry are a complete visual language.

04

Colour Theory

The physics of feeling

Colour is never neutral. Itten, Albers, Munsell — the study of how colours act on each other and on the eye. Palette is the first decision and the most consequential.

How I Work
01

Research & Discovery

Understanding the context: what the project is for, who it is for, and what it needs to say. Good design begins with good questions.

02

Concept & Exploration

Sketching, colour exploration, typographic experiments. Finding the idea before executing it. The unexpected path is usually the right one.

03

Refinement

Narrowing from many to one. Adjusting until the solution feels inevitable — not clever, not decorative, but exactly right.

04

Delivery

Prepared files, brand guidelines, or print-ready artwork. Clear documentation so the work continues to function beyond our collaboration.

LB

Visual identity · Illustration · Print design
